titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Alvah Hovey Papers
titlestmt.author: Andover Newton Theological School and Yale Divinity Library staff

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 28, 2018 at 23:01

did.abstract: This collection documents the work of Alvah Hovey (1820-1903) who taught at Newton Theological Institution in Newton Centre, Massachusetts from 1849 to 1903 and was President of the Institution from 1868-1898.

chronitemset.event: Graduated from Dartmouth College, New Hampshire
Assistant Instructor, Hebrew, Newton Theological Institution
Acting Professor, Biblical Literature and Interpretation, NTI; married to Augusta Rice, 1852
Professor, Theology and Christian Ethics, NTI
Professor, General Introduction and Apologetics, NTI
Acting Professor, Biblical Interpretation, N.T., NTI

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Andover Newton Theological School, 2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Alvah Hovey Papers, 1842-1903, document the professional work of Hovey. The collection includes autobiographical essays, diaries, correspondence, sermons, addresses, lectures, essays, unpublished and published literary manuscripts, student lecture notes from Dartmouth College and Newton Theological Institution, notes on Biblical texts and theological subjects, newspaper clippings, and association items.

Included in Series II, Correspondence, are letters of other 19th century Baptist clergy and educators including Elisha B. Andrews, George D. Boardman, John A. Broadus, Charles R. Brown, Henry S. Burrage, Ernest D. Burton, William N. Clarke, William H.P. Faunce, Shailer Mathews, George E. Merrill, George W. Northrup, Edwards A. Park, George D. B. Pepper, Henry E. Robins, Ezekiel G. Robinson, Artemus W. Sawyer, Augustus H. Strong, and Henry G. Weston. A more detailed listing and name index for the correspondence are included in the original finding aid for the collection, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.
The documents in Series VIII, Association Items, are not in Hovey's hand; and their relation to other material in the collection is unclear. This series includes letters and literary manuscripts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in ten series:

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Portions of the collection are available in microfilm format.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Alvah Hovey Papers (RG 288), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library

did.unittitle: Autobiographical essay - two drafts, partly in Augusta Hovey's hand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See the original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton, for a complete listing of correspondence and name index.

did.unittitle: Including with Jonas Terry, Arabella D. Skinner
Including with Irah Chase, Artemus Sawyer, John B. Foster, O.S. Stearns, Edwards A. Park
Including with E.G. Robinson, Hattie Carpenter, Adele M. Fielde, A.H. Strong, John C. Foster, George P. Fisher
Including with W. Henry Green, Augusta Hovey, James B. Angell, Josiah Strong, G.D. B. Pepper
Including with Walter Rauschenbusch, Charles Rufus Brown, R. H. Dana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See the original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton, for a full listing of sermon texts and titles of addresses.

did.unittitle: List of sermons preached at Gloucester, Massachusetts 1848-1849
Undated sermons arranged by text, Exodus through 2 Timothy
Undated sermons arranged by text, I Peter through Jude

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lectures are arranged according to the outline of general subjects provided by Hovey in his Syllabus of Theological Propaedeutics (n.d.).

did.unittitle: Lectures on Biblical Theology and Historical Theology
Lectures on Historical Theology and Systematic Theology
Lectures on Systematic Theology, Practical Theology, and Apologetic Theology
Unpublished essays arranged by title, "The Atonement, or self-sacrifice of Christ" through "Paul's Desire"
Unpublished essays arranged by title, "The Purpose of God as to Creation, etc." through "The Work of Christ in Saving Men"
Biography of the Rev'd. Chapin Howard Carpenter
Commentary on the Epistle to the Galatians. vol. 8, An American Commentary on the New Testament . Philadelphia: American Baptist Publication Society, 1890. (in Augusta Hovey's hand)
Studies in Ethics and Religion . Boston: Silver, Burdett, & Co., Pulishers, 1892. (incomplete--missing pages 299-301, 454-588)
Barnas Sears: A Christian Educator, his Making and Work. New York: Silver, Burdett & Company, 1902. rough draft of chapter four.
Christian Science and Mind-Cure (from Studies in Ethics and Religion ); "Stapfer on the Resurrection of Jesus Christ," American Journal of Theology IV (1904); 536-554.
The State of Men after Death . Philadelphia: American Baptist Publication Society, n.d.
Printed copies of articles with manuscript notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes taken while a student at Dartmough College

did.unittitle: Notebooks and notes on the Bible
1 volume notes on commentaries on the Bible
Letters to H.S. Burrage in response to invitations to attend events honoring Hovey's 50th anniversary at Newton Theological Institution
Account book, April 1851-1868 (contains lists of books purchased by AH)
Manuscripts in Hovey's hand; translations of others' works, etc.
Miscellaneous letters neither to nor from Hovey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton, for a full listing of letters.

did.unittitle: Atheism the only Alternative of Evangelical Christianity (2 folders)
Alonzo Bunker, The Government of Burma and the Baptist Mission in Mission schools in Burma. The Case Stated
The Canon of the Old Testament, including the Apocrypha (2 folders)
John Hall. Theology and Missions (2 folders)
Schulze. The Son of Man and the Logos. selections translated by Sylvester Burnham, NTI 1873 (9 folders)
A Memoir of the Life and Times of the Rev. Isaac Backus, A.M. by Alvah Hovey, DD, Professor of Christian Theology in Newton Theological Institution
A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles by Hackett and Hovey
Miscellaneous writings, including The Newton Theological Institution since 1872
Material relating to Hovey's resignation, illness, and death
Miscellaneous correspondence, map of Newton Theological Institute campus
